briefdescription.para: Number of regions managed by LLEXT.
List of memory regions stored or referenced in the LLEXT subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: This enum lists the different types of memory regions that are used by the LLEXT subsystem. The names match common ELF file section names; but note that at load time multiple ELF sections with similar flags may be merged together into a single memory region.

briefdescription.para: Entry point function signature for an extension.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the llext, or NULL if not found

detaileddescription.para: Calls a provided callback function for each registered extension or until the callback function returns a non-0 value.

parameterdescription.para: a private argument to be provided to the callback function

simplesect.para: the value returned by the last callback invocation

memberdef.argsstring: (struct llext_loader *loader, const char *name, struct llext **ext, const struct llext_load_param *ldr_parm)
memberdef.name: llext_load

detaileddescription.para: Loads relevant ELF data into memory and provides a structure to work with it.

parameterdescription.para: An extension loader that provides input data and context
A string identifier for the extension
Pointer to the newly allocated llext structure
Optional advanced load parameters (may be NULL)

simplesect.para: the previous extension use count on success, or a negative error code.

briefdescription.para: Free any inspection-related memory for the specified loader and extension.

detaileddescription.para: This is only required if inspection data was requested at load time by setting llext_load_param::keep_section_info; otherwise, this call will be a no-op.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code.

briefdescription.para: Calls bringup functions for an extension.

detaileddescription.para:
Must be called before accessing any symbol in the extension. Will execute the extension's own setup functions in the caller context.
llext_bootstrap
ext
Extension to initialize.
0 on success, or a negative error code.
-EFAULT
A relocation issue was detected

briefdescription.para: Calls teardown functions for an extension.

detaileddescription.para:
Will execute the extension's own cleanup functions in the caller context. After this function completes, the extension is no longer usable and must be fully unloaded with llext_unload.
llext_bootstrap
ext
Extension to de-initialize.
0 on success, or a negative error code.
-EFAULT
A relocation issue was detected

briefdescription.para: Bring up, execute, and teardown an extension.

detaileddescription.para: Calls the extension's own setup functions, an additional entry point and the extension's cleanup functions in the current thread context.
This is a convenient wrapper around llext_bringup and llext_teardown that matches the k_thread_entry_t signature, so it can be directly started as a new user or kernel thread via k_thread_create.

parameterdescription.para: Extension to execute. Passed as p1 in k_thread_create.
Main entry point of the thread after performing extension setup. Passed as p2 in k_thread_create.
Argument passed to entry_fn. Passed as p3 in k_thread_create.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct llext *ext, bool is_init, void *buf, size_t size)
memberdef.name: llext_get_fn_table

briefdescription.para: Get pointers to setup or cleanup functions for an extension.

detaileddescription.para: This syscall can be used to get the addresses of all the functions that have to be called for full extension setup or cleanup.

parameterdescription.para: true to get functions to be called at setup time, false to get the cleanup ones.
Buffer to store the function pointers in. Can be NULL to only get the minimum required size.
Allocated size of the buffer in bytes.

simplesect.para: the size used by the array in bytes, or a negative error code.

parameterdescription.para: Array does not fit in the allocated buffer

briefdescription.para: Find the address for an arbitrary symbol.

detaileddescription.para: Searches for a symbol address, either in the list of symbols exported by the main Zephyr binary or in an extension's symbol table.

parameterdescription.para: Symbol table to lookup symbol in, or NULL to search in the main Zephyr symbol table

simplesect.para: the address of symbol in memory, or NULL if not found

detaileddescription.para: Expects a symbol representing a void fn(void) style function exists and may be called.

parameterdescription.para: Function name (exported symbol) in the extension

briefdescription.para: Add an extension to a memory domain.

detaileddescription.para: Allows an extension to be executed in user mode threads when memory protection hardware is enabled by adding memory partitions covering the extension's memory regions to a memory domain.

parameterdescription.para: Extension to add to a domain
Memory domain to add partitions to

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code.

parameterdescription.para: Option CONFIG_USERSPACE is not enabled or supported

memberdef.argsstring: (struct llext_loader *ldr, struct llext *ext, elf_rela_t *rel, const elf_shdr_t *shdr)
memberdef.name: arch_elf_relocate

detaileddescription.para: ELF files include sections describing a series of relocations, which are instructions on how to rewrite opcodes given the actual placement of some symbolic data such as a section, function, or object. These relocations are architecture specific and each architecture supporting LLEXT must implement this. Arguments sym_base_addr, sym_name can be computed from the sym parameter, but these parameters are provided redundantly to increase efficiency.

parameterdescription.para: Header of the ELF section currently being located

briefdescription.para: Locates an ELF section in the file.

detaileddescription.para: Searches for a section by name in the ELF file and returns its offset.

simplesect.para: the section offset or a negative error code

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct llext_loader *loader, const struct llext *ext, const char *search_name, elf_shdr_t *shdr)
memberdef.name: llext_get_section_header

briefdescription.para: Extract ELF section header by name.

detaileddescription.para: Searches for a section by name in the ELF file and retrieves its full header.
Use the provided memory block as the LLEXT heap at runtime.

parameterdescription.para: Size of memory region, in bytes

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code.

parameterdescription.para: Option CONFIG_LLEXT_HEAP_DYNAMIC is not enabled or supported, or it is and option CONFIG_HARVARD is enabled

briefdescription.para: Initialize LLEXT heap dynamically for Harvard architecture.

detaileddescription.para: Use the provided memory blocks as the LLEXT heaps at runtime.

parameterdescription.para: Size of instruction memory region, in bytes
Size of data memory region, in bytes

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code.

parameterdescription.para: Option CONFIG_LLEXT_HEAP_DYNAMIC is not enabled or supported, or it is and option CONFIG_HARVARD is not enabled

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or a negative error code.

parameterdescription.para: Option CONFIG_LLEXT_HEAP_DYNAMIC is not enabled or supported

briefdescription.para: Relink dependencies to prepare for suspend.

detaileddescription.para: For suspend-resume use-cases, when LLEXT context should be saved in a non-volatile buffer, the user can save most LLEXT support data, but they have to use llext_restore() to re-allocate objects, which will also have to restore dependency pointers. To make sure dependency saving and restoring is done consistently, we provide a helper function for the former too.

simplesect.para: this is a part of an experimental API, it WILL change in the future! Its availability depends on CONFIG_LLEXT_EXPERIMENTAL, which is disabled by default.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct llext **ext, struct llext_loader **ldr, unsigned int n_ext)
memberdef.name: llext_restore

briefdescription.para: Restore LLEXT context from saved data.

detaileddescription.para: During suspend the user has saved all the extension and loader descriptors and related objects and called llext_relink_dependency() to prepare dependency pointers. When resuming llext_alloc_metadata() has to be used to re-allocate all the objects, therefore the user needs support from LLEXT core to accomplish that. This function takes arrays of pointers to saved copies of extensions and loaders as arguments and re-allocates all the objects, while also adding them to the global extension list. At the same time it relinks dependency pointers to newly allocated extensions.

simplesect.para: this is a part of an experimental API, it WILL change in the future! Its availability depends on CONFIG_LLEXT_EXPERIMENTAL, which is disabled by default.

parameterdescription.para: Extension pointer array - replaced with re-allocated copies
Array of loader pointers to restore section maps

briefdescription.para: Maximum length of an extension name.
